summary.narrative: Katie Hobbs, Arizona's 30th Governor (Democrat, inaugurated January 2023), has focused on healthcare access, water security, education funding, and border management in her first term. Her signature accomplishments include brokering a historic 2023 Colorado River water conservation agreement, announcing $30 million in medical debt forgiveness (March 2024), pausing capital punishment, and issuing 63 vetoes of Republican-led legislation in her first legislative session. She has also launched a reelection campaign emphasizing 'real results' and announced proposals to expand the Arizona Promise Scholarship Program to undocumented students and cut middle-class taxes through executive order.
summary.grade_rationale: Hobbs has achieved significant outcomes on water security and medical debt forgiveness, demonstrating executive capacity and cross-state negotiation. However, many policy commitments remain in early stages or contingent on legislative action in a Republican-controlled chamber, limiting implementation reach. Education and tax cut initiatives show mixed progress.

promises.normalized: Broker Colorado River water conservation agreement
Expand Arizona Promise Scholarship to undocumented students
Cut middle-class taxes via executive action

data_gaps: Specific dollar amounts or timelines for education funding extension (Prop 123)
Details on middle-class tax cuts (target income thresholds, estimated savings)
Status and completion date for school facility safety executive orders
Measurable outcomes from National Guard border deployment (December 2023)
Campaign platform positions on healthcare, housing, and economic development beyond scattered mentions
Comprehensive list of bills vetoed and legislative priorities for 2024-2025 sessions
